_units: median wall-clock in milliseconds from pytest-benchmark default config (warmup absorbed)
_notes: Post-T30 Step 3 baseline. The cumsum FFI in `crates/polars-metal-mlx-sys` now passes input/output as `rust::Slice` (thin pointer+length) instead of per-element `cxx::CxxVector` push/iter loops. With 10M-row inputs the Rust-side push of 10M u8s plus the iter-copy of 10M u32s was costing ~40ms per call on its own; eliminating those loops drops filter_simple from 117.2ms to 77.8ms (-34%) and brings the Metal/CPU ratio for filter_simple under 9x for the first time. The internal MLX `array(ptr, shape, dtype)` constructor still copies bytes into MLX-managed memory and we memcpy the scan result back into the caller's slice — those two memcpys are the only data-touching passes left in the cumsum call. Pushing the FFI cost lower than this requires the M2 MLX-over-MTLBuffer rewrite tracked in docs/open-questions.md.

tpch_q1_modified._notes: Boolean l_returnflag/l_linestatus (encoder 128-bit budget). Post-Phase 6: dispatch_build routes ≥500K rows through A1 (GPU partitioned-hash build). At 4 groups the build phase savings are small; tied with CPU at the engine level. Larger absolute speedup vs M2 baseline (309ms → 37ms) is from Phase 3 fused-agg kernel + warmup; A1 is harmless here, dominant at high cardinality. Measured 2026-05-26 (M2 Ultra), 27/13 rounds.

tpch_q1_modified_32bit._notes: All-GPU 32-bit variant. Bool keys + i32/f32 values; fused agg path. Same story as tpch_q1_modified — A1 active above 500K rows but build phase is tiny at 4 groups, so tied with CPU at engine level. Measured 2026-05-26, 38/33 rounds.

tpch_q1_modified_32bit_high_card._notes: Q1-shaped query grouped by l_part_class (~1024 distinct groups). Post-Phase 6: dispatch_build routes through A1 above 500K rows. **Metal 18% faster than CPU** — A1's build-phase savings (CPU HashMap ~127ms → A1 ~30ms at 10M × 1024 groups in microbench) are visible at the query level. This is the win the routing was designed for: high-cardinality groupby beats CPU. Measured 2026-05-26, 14/7 rounds.

tpch_q1_canonical._notes: TPC-H Q1's actual schema: Utf8 l_returnflag (3 vals) × l_linestatus (2 vals) = 6 unique key combos. Filter on Date l_shipdate <= 1998-09-02 cuts ~5%. Phase 7 (Utf8 dtype) unlocked this fixture; the engine now routes the groupby through fused-agg + A1, but at 6 unique groups the build phase is small and the bottleneck shifts to CPU-routed filter + sort. Ratio 1.005 (tied) — does NOT meet the plan's original 0.7 gate, but that gate was set without measuring what fraction of Q1 was actually filter+sort. The unmet gate is an *honest* measurement input for the M3 retrospective: Q1's intrinsic low cardinality means dispatch routing alone isn't enough; filter and/or sort must move to GPU (Phase 10) to push the ratio below 1. Measured 2026-05-27, 14/16 rounds.
